audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for wilderness warnings, temptation, cup/table participation, idol table, liberty and conscience, do all to Divine glory.

verses.restored: For I do not want you to be unaware, siblings, that our fathers were all under the cloud and all passed through the sea,
and all were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea,
and all ate the same spiritual food,
and all drank the same spiritual drink; for they were drinking from the spiritual rock following them, and the rock was Messiah.
Nevertheless, with most of them the Divine was not pleased, for they were scattered in the wilderness.
These things became patterns for us, so that we would not be desirers of harmful things, just as they also desired.
Do not become idolaters, as some of them; just as it is written: The people sat down to eat and drink and rose up to play.
Nor should we practice sexual immorality/misuse, as some of them practiced, and twenty-three thousand fell in one day.
Nor should we test Messiah, as some of them tested, and were destroyed by serpents.
Nor grumble, as some of them grumbled, and were destroyed by the destroyer.
These things happened to them as patterns, and were written for our admonition, upon whom the completions of the ages have come.
Therefore let the one thinking to stand watch lest one fall.

No testing has taken you except what is human; but trustworthy is the Divine, who will not allow you to be tested beyond what you are able, but with the testing will also make the way out, so that you may be able to bear it.
Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
I speak as to discerning ones; judge what I say.
The cup of blessing that we bless, is it not fellowship/participation of the blood of Messiah? The bread that we break, is it not fellowship/participation of the body of Messiah?
Because there is one bread, we the many are one body, for we all share from the one bread.
See Israel according to flesh: are not those eating the sacrifices sharers of the altar?
What then do I say? That idol-offering is anything, or that an idol is anything?
No, but that what the nations sacrifice, they sacrifice to daimonia and not to the Divine; I do not want you to become sharers with daimonia.
You are not able to drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of daimonia; you are not able to share the table of the Lord and the table of daimonia.
Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?
All things are permitted, but not all things are beneficial. All things are permitted, but not all things build.
Let no one seek one's own, but that of the other.
Eat everything sold in the meat market, examining nothing because of conscience,
for the earth is the Lord's and its fullness.
If one of the untrusting invites you and you wish to go, eat everything set before you, examining nothing because of conscience.
But if someone says to you, This is offered in sacrifice, do not eat because of that one who disclosed it and because of conscience.
I mean not your own conscience, but the other's. For why is my freedom judged by another's conscience?
If I partake with thanks, why am I blasphemed over what I give thanks for?
Therefore, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, do all into glory of the Divine.
Become without stumbling-block to Judeans and Greeks and the assembly of the Divine,
just as I also please all in all things, not seeking my own advantage but that of the many, so that they may be saved/healed.

translation_notes: Daimonia is left transliterated to avoid over-systematizing later demonology; the issue is real spiritual participation at rival tables.
The Lord's table creates one body, so liberty must be interpreted by communion and neighbor-love.
